sprint: Traveller Save Notice — Repositioned Out of the Object Strip's Way, Copy Corrected
task: A real screenshot of a live Workspace showed the Traveller Save Notice (position:fixed, anchored to the viewport bottom-center) visually colliding with the Object Strip's own cards and scroll-arrow buttons: 'we need to reposition this. or allow traveller to reposition this. also why we are saying claim magic card as there is no other way to claim the card.' Two fixes. (1) Reposition: js/travellerSaveNotice.js's _ensureDom() now mounts the notice as a normal, in-flow element -- the very FIRST child of .right-sidebar -- instead of appending to document.body with fixed positioning; .right-sidebar already has overflow-y:auto and the notice sits ahead of #contextPanelRoot, so it is never wiped by ContextPanel.refresh()'s own frequent panelRoot.innerHTML='' calls, which only touch #contextPanelRoot's own children. A first attempt inserted the notice 'right after .tabs' (mirroring where ContextPanel.init() inserts #contextPanelRoot) -- caught, via a Playwright DOM-order check, as a real boot-order-dependent bug: since TravellerSaveNotice.refresh()'s first call runs before ContextPanel.init() in the real boot sequence, the notice would insert itself right after .tabs first, and ContextPanel.init()'s own later insertBefore(panelRoot, tabsEl.nextSibling) call would then insert #contextPanelRoot BEFORE the already-present notice -- silently pushing the safety notice below the entire Context Panel instead of above it. Fixed by dropping the .tabs-relative logic in favour of always inserting as .right-sidebar's unconditional first child. css/style.css's .traveller-save-notice* rules were rewritten from a floating toast into a normal sidebar card (a stacked icon+text+dismiss row, a full-width Publish Now button beneath) -- the same recurring button:not(...){width:100%;margin-bottom:12px} global-cascade override discipline already fixed repeatedly elsewhere (Object Strip cards, the Publish Studio close button, Doodle's pen swatches) still applies, just with vertical-layout widths now. (2) Copy correction: reworded from '...publish your adventure (or claim your Magic Card) to make sure it's kept safe' -- factually misleading, since claiming a Magic Card only ever happens as an automatic consequence of a first successful Publish (MagicCard.shouldOfferAwakening(), fired from js/publishStudio.js's _finalizePublish), never an independent action -- to 'You're creating as a Traveller. Publish your adventure to make sure it's kept safe.', naming exactly one real action. Verified via a new 12-assertion Playwright suite confirming the notice mounts inside .right-sidebar (not document.body), is genuinely not position:fixed, has real non-zero layout size, does not visually overlap the Object Strip's bounding rect, is the true first child of .right-sidebar, the corrected copy no longer mentions 'claim your Magic Card' while still mentioning Publish, and both buttons remain fully functional from the new location -- plus the original 15-assertion suite (its own test helper needed one matching fix: it previously only hid #creationFlowOverlay without removing body.creation-flow-active, an incomplete stand-in for the real _finish()/_closeOverlay() sequence that never mattered for a fixed-position toast outside #app's own display:none gating, but does matter now that the notice lives inside .right-sidebar, a real #app descendant) -- all 15 pass unchanged. Full regression across the entire existing Gateway/Magic-Card Playwright suite (gw_final_video_test.js 15/15, gw_returning_unified_test.js 18/18, gw_completion_check.js, gateway_skip_check.js, gw_myprojects_clear_check.js 5/5, gw_smoke_handoff.js 6/6, gw_decline_choice_test.js 11/11) and both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected) pass unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/travellerSaveNotice.js and css/style.css.
